demos.title: NEMA 23 belt-driven linear axis
demos.summary: A reusable first assembly demo that exercises motor mounting, pulley/belt transmission, linear guidance, carriage, homing, AssemblyGraph mates, and a one-axis URDF export. It is intentionally not a rated motion stage.

kinematic_model.axis: +X travel axis to be resolved from selected rail datum
kinematic_model.note: Travel limits, speed, force, and acceleration are intentionally absent until selected hardware and application loads are known.

required_inputs: payload mass, CoG, and external loads
travel, speed, acceleration, and duty cycle
exact motor/driver/guideway/belt/pulley part numbers and controlled drawings
structural material/process and mounting context
required accuracy, repeatability, environment, and safety context
verification_gates: Resolve all candidate components and interface drawings.
Run motor, belt, guideway, structure, fastener, and thermal calculations with reviewed assumptions.
Check full-stroke collision, belt path, service access, and end-stop/hard-stop strategy.
Validate AssemblyGraph frame transforms and prismatic URDF model.
Produce a BOM that marks unresolved candidate lines and does not authorize procurement.
exports: FCStd assembly and per-part source FeatureGraphs
STEP assembly with selected/reviewed physical parts only
URDF/Xacro and collision mesh after frame/property verification
demos.agent_guidance: Start with this demo. Present it as a design walkthrough until all exact component records and verification evidence replace the candidate/illustrative records.

demos.title: Electric parallel-jaw gripper
demos.summary: A custom end-effector demo that exercises a coupled two-jaw AssemblyGraph, custom jaws, guideway/drive integration, gripper tool frames, and mimic-joint export limitations.

kinematic_model.note: Physical drive mechanism and joint limits are unselected.
URDF mimic relationship is not evidence of synchronized hardware or grasp force balance.

required_inputs: workpiece geometry/material and grasp task
jaw stroke, force, speed, and cycle life
exact guideway, screw/nut/bearing/motor/controller data
robot or fixture host interface
pinch-point, failed-power, and safety context
material/process and cleaning/environment constraints
verification_gates: Resolve the physical synchronization/drive mechanism and candidate components.
Calculate force transmission, screw/nut/bearing loading, jaw/body stiffness, fasteners, and duty life.
Check open, closed, intermediate, and workpiece collision states.
Verify tool-centre frame and upstream payload/moment limits.
Validate the URDF mimic approximation in the target simulator and document its limitation.
exports: FCStd assembly and individual body/jaw FeatureGraphs
STEP assembly after exact components are selected
URDF/Xacro plus collision mesh with explicit mimic caveat
demos.agent_guidance: Build this second. It demonstrates that OrionFlow handles custom parts, purchased components, coupled kinematics, and end-effector-specific verification rather than only simple single parts.

demos.title: Two-axis camera or LiDAR pan-tilt head
demos.summary: A two-revolute-joint sensor-head concept that proves rotary joint frames, payload mounting, cable service envelopes, sensor field-of-view constraints, and URDF export.

kinematic_model.note: Range, speed, stops, zero, and torque are unselected.
Cable torque, sensor CoG, and motion limits must be resolved before actuator sizing.

required_inputs: exact camera/LiDAR model and drawing
sensor mass, CoG, field of view/scan plane, and thermal needs
pan/tilt range, speed, acceleration, and environmental context
exact actuators/controllers/encoders
cable/harness part numbers and motion life
mounting platform and safety context
verification_gates: Resolve sensor frame, aperture keep-outs, mounting pattern, and cable exits.
Calculate joint torque/acceleration including sensor inertia, gravity, cable forces, and external loads.
Check all permitted joint poses for collisions, field-of-view obstruction, cable bend/twist, and serviceability.
Validate actuator load limits, joint limits, zero poses, and URDF frame transforms.
Mark calibration and vibration testing as required evidence.
exports: STEP only after selected component geometry is verified
URDF/Xacro, collision mesh, and joint-frame report
demos.agent_guidance: Build this third. It is a compact, highly visual demo for robotics-specific geometry, coordinate frames, sensor integration, and cable routing.

demos.title: Modular BLDC plus strain-wave actuator
demos.summary: A high-value actuator module concept that combines one source-specific Harmonic Drive component set with candidate motor, encoder, brake, custom housing, and output flange. It demonstrates strict provenance boundaries inside a real multi-part robotics assembly.

kinematic_model.axis: +Z output axis to be resolved from current gear drawing
kinematic_model.note: The listed gear catalogue data is source-specific, but the integrated actuator's limits, life, and zero pose are not yet verified.

required_inputs: current Harmonic Drive controlled drawing and integration restrictions
selected BLDC motor/brake/encoder/controller drawings and data
robot-link loads, mass/CoG/inertia, duty cycle, and target motion
housing material/process, thermal path, sealing, and service requirements
cable/connector architecture and safety/risk context
calibration, acceptance-test, and lifecycle requirements
verification_gates: Retrieve and attach current controlled drawings for every selected supplier part.
Calculate motor/gear speed-torque duty, efficiency, thermal behaviour, fatigue/life, and external output bearing loads with qualified review.
Verify housing, fastener, bearing, flange, and cable load paths.
Check full joint motion/collision, output zero/feedback mapping, and target URDF model.
Do not label the actuator production-ready until system-level test and safety evidence are approved.
exports: FCStd multi-part actuator assembly and custom-part FeatureGraphs
STEP package with selected supplier geometry subject to redistribution rights
URDF/Xacro joint with verified physical properties only
demos.agent_guidance: Build this fourth. It is the strongest provenance demo: the reducer's catalogue facts are source-specific, while every integration claim remains explicitly candidate or illustrative until evidence is added.
